en.extract:
The yellow-bibbed lory (Lorius chlorocercus) is a species of parrot in the family Psittaculidae. It is endemic to the southern Solomon Islands.


Description
The yellow-bibbed lory is 28 cm (11 in) long. It is mostly red with black on top of the head and green wings. It has a yellow transverse band on the upper chest and a crescent-shaped black patch on each side of the neck. It has blue/green thighs and dark grey legs. It has an orange-red beak, dark grey eyerings, and orange irises. Under its wings the bird has blue feathers.


Range and habitat
The yellow-bibbed lory is endemic to the eastern Solomon Islands. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


Relationship with humans
Yellow-bibbed Lories mimic a wide range of sounds, including the human voice, in other words they are 'talking parrots'. Providing a permit is obtained, it is exempt from export prohibition under the Solomon Islands Wildlife Protection and Management Act (1998)


References


Cited texts
Forshaw, Joseph M. (2006). Parrots of the World; an Identification Guide. Illustrated by Frank Knight. Princeton University Press. ISBN 0-691-09251-6.

The '''yellow-bibbed lory''' ('''''Lorius chlorocercus''''') is a species of [[parrot]] in the family [[Psittaculidae]].<ref name = zoonomen>{{cite web | url =http://www.zoonomen.net/avtax/psit.html |title = Zoological Nomenclature Resource: Psittaciformes (Version 9.022) |date =2009-03-28|publisher = zoonomen.net }}</ref><ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 17.">Forshaw (2006). plate 17.</ref> It is endemic to the southern [[Solomon Islands]].

==Description==
The yellow-bibbed lory is 28 cm (11 in) long. It is mostly red with black on top of the head and green wings. It has a yellow transverse band on the upper chest and a crescent-shaped black patch on each side of the neck. It has blue/green thighs and dark grey legs. It has an orange-red beak, dark grey eyerings, and orange irises. Under its wings the bird has blue feathers.<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 17."/>

<gallery>
File:Yellow-bibbed-Lory-lorius-chlorocercus.jpg|Front
File:Yellow-bibbed-Lory.jpg|Side view
File:Yellow-bibbed-Lory03.jpg|Side view
File:Lorius chlorocercus-20040821B.jpg|From above
File:Lorius chlorocercus on cage.jpg|Underside
</gallery>

==Range and habitat==
The yellow-bibbed lory is endemic to the eastern [[Solomon Islands]].<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 17."/> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

==Relationship with humans==

Yellow-bibbed Lories mimic a wide range of sounds, including the human voice, in other words they are 'talking parrots'.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Schroeder|first1=Dick|title=The Yellow-Bibbed Lory|url=http://www.birdchannel.com/bird-species/find-the-right-bird/yellow-bibbed-lory.aspx|website=Bird Channel|access-date=3 June 2015|archive-date=24 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140224025451/http://www.birdchannel.com/bird-species/find-the-right-bird/yellow-bibbed-lory.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> Providing a permit is obtained, it is exempt from export prohibition under the Solomon Islands Wildlife Protection and Management Act (1998)<ref>{{cite book|last1=Shepherd|first1=C.R.|last2=Stengel|first2=C.J.|last3=Nijman|first3=V.|title=The Export and Re-export of CITES-listed Birds from the Solomon Islands|date=2012|publisher=TRAFFIC Southeast Asia|location=Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia|isbn=978-983-3393-35-0|url=https://awsassets.panda.org/downloads/sb_report__final_1.pdf}}</ref>
